title: put() unit tests using mock kube HTTP layer
description:
What: Write unit tests for K8sBackend::put() covering create-or-patch semantics, key targeting, and error paths. No real Kubernetes cluster required.

Algorithm:
1. Reuse the mock kube HTTP layer infrastructure established in the get() tests bead (W3A).
2. Implement test cases:
   a. test_put_creates_new_secret: mock returns 404 on PATCH, then 201 on POST (create); result is Ok(()); verify POST body contains correct base64 value for key "value". Oracle: the request body captured by the mock.
   b. test_put_patches_existing_key: mock returns 200 on PATCH; ?key=password; result is Ok(()); verify PATCH body contains only the target key. Oracle: request body.
   c. test_put_no_key_replaces_data: mock returns 200 on PUT (replace); no ?key=; result is Ok(()); verify body's .data contains single entry "value". Oracle: request body.
   d. test_put_forbidden: mock returns 403; result is Err(SecretError::Unavailable) or Err(SecretError::Backend). Assert specific variant.
   e. test_put_with_key_preserves_other_keys: confirm PATCH body (Merge) does NOT include other keys (only the patched key); other keys remain because merge semantics preserve them. Oracle: PATCH body has exactly one key in .data.
3. For request body inspection, capture the request in the mock service and assert on the serialized JSON.

Defensive programming rules:
- Never assert on raw secret bytes being printed — only compare deserialized request body against known expected bytes.
- Error-path tests must assert the specific SecretError variant.
- Mock must not call out to a real cluster.

Subagent instructions: Spawn research subagent to read W3A implementation before writing to reuse the mock infrastructure. Spawn validation subagent to run cargo test -p secretx-k8s.

Tests listed above in Algorithm step 2.
